course_description: SLAT7899 focuses on three crucial components of our daily social interaction: language, culture and communication, and explores the role that the use of language plays in social interaction and how meaning is created in our communication. More specifically, the course examines language as culture and the pragmatics of social interaction. It discusses aspects of language structure and use, interpersonal behaviour, communication patterns, multimodal aspects of communication, identity, (im)polilteness, and a range of associated topics.

course_aims:
In SLAT7899, we willﾠ explore language, culture and social interaction in terms of theory and analysis. The course approaches various topics from a discourse analytical perspective, focussing on a micro-level analysis of what happens when people communicate, primarily how the meaning is created and what social actions are accomplished in interaction.

The course considers language as a cultural vehicle. It is designed to complement course offerings in language and linguistic studies, communication studies, culture studies, translation studies, and in other intellectual disciplines which involve the passage of information across cultural boundaries. Students are expected:

learning_outcomes.description: Identify linguistic and discourse features in communication, including those contributing to cultural and societal diversity.
Effectively apply the concepts and theories studied in the course to interaction analysis.
Critically examine academic literature on the course content.
Effectively communicate the aspects of social interaction, both in writing and verbally.

assessment_summary.weight: 45% (15% per analysis - your best 3 out of 4)

assessment_details.title: In-class discussions
assessment_details.task_description: In Weeks 2-12, in the tutorials there will be individual and group discussions of the assigned readings and data analysis. Students will need to prepare for the tutorials by (1) having done the course readings and (2) revisiting the lecture content. Bringing your own notes is highly encouraged. Contributions to the discussions will be marked as follows (1 mark per week) : 1 mark - Student shows clear engagement with and critical understanding of the readings and the weekly content 0.5 marks - Student shows some engagement with and understanding of the readings and the weekly content 0 marks - Student does not engage and, thus, shows no to very limited engagement with and understanding of the readings and the weekly content Generative AI & MT Statement This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted . Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This assessment will be done in-person with no submission necessary.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
This assessment task is designed to assess students’ preparation, engagement and active participation in class (group) discussions, which is difficult to recreate outside the classroom environment. Each class includes specific activities that allow teaching team to track students’ participation and engagement to fulfil marking criteria outlined above. These activities are made available to students regardless of their presence in class (via Blackboard course site). Furthermore, there will be 11 opportunities for weekly in-class discussions (1 mark per week) and 9 to 11 marks will count as a maximum final percentage (15%).

assessment_details.title: Critical Analysis
assessment_details.weight: 45% (15% per analysis - your best 3 out of 4)
assessment_details.due_date: WEEK 5 - Critical Analysis 1 - 27/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 8 - Critical Analysis 2 - 24/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 10 - Critical Analysis 3 - 8/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 12 - Critical Analysis 4 - 22/05/2026 5:00 pm
assessment_details.task_description: Critical Analysis Weight: 45% (15% per analysis; your best 3 analyses out of 4 will count towards the final mark ) Due: 5 pm on Friday in Weeks 5, 8, 10 and 12. Length: 500 words (excluding references) for written analyses; 5-7 min. for oral analyses In Weeks 3, 6, 9 & 11, you will have access to the interactional data in the Assessment folder on Blackboard that you will need to analyse in terms of what is covered in the weeks that correspond to the analysis: Critical Analysis 1: Weeks 3, 4 & 5- written submission Critical Analysis 2: Weeks 6,7 & 8 - video submission Critical Analysis 3: Weeks 9 & 10 - written submission Critical Analysis 4: Weeks 11 & 12 - video submission In your analysis, you will need to identify linguistic and discourse features in the data relevant to the weeks indicated in the task; explain those features and what happens in the data in terms of the relevant course concepts; support your analysis with the relevant readings from the course (from the reading list, plus those covered in lecture and tutorial, where relevant; 3-5 references). Do not use outside-the-course additional readings. Generative AI & MT Statement This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Part A: Interaction project (Written submission)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Interaction Project This multi-stage assessment task is designed so that you could apply your knowledge of the pragmatics of social interaction to authentic data – a real-life phone conversation. This task mirrors real-world practices in the fields such as discourse analysis, pragmatics, conversation analysis, interpersonal and intercultural communication where analysing authentic interaction is essential. In Week 4, you will have access to three to five 30-minute phone conversations* in the Assessment folder on Blackboard that you will have to listen to in order to become famliar with the data. In Week 6, you will need to choose one of those phone conversations that you will use for your project. The choice of each phone conversation will be limited, i.e. all the students cannot select the same conversation. This means that you need to identify more than one phone conversation of your interest prior to Week 6. The final data allocation will be made on a first-come, first-served basis via a live document. Students not present in class will be randomly assigned one of the available recordings. *Students who wish to use the conversational data that they audio (video) record themselves will need to contact the course coordinator by Week 3. They will need to collect the data (no ethical clearance needed) by Week 5 and share it with the course coordinator to obtain the approval to use it for the Interaction Project assessment. IMPORTANT : Start working with the data early in the semester! Note that it is NOT a group project, which means that your extracts selection and transcriptions must reflect your own individual work. Interaction Project - Written Submission (40%) Deadline : Week 13, Friday, 5 pm Length : 2000-2400 words (excluding extracts and references) Your written submission must include the following sections and 15-20 references (for more information, see the template on Blackboard): Introduction (explanation of why it is important to research social interaction - provide examples from empirical studies); Literature review (research done in relation to the identified concept + aims of this project); Data description (source, participants, context, etc.); Analysis & discussion (here you need to focus on 1 concept from the course that became visible in the recorded interaction): provide in-depth analyses of 2 extracts. Conclusion (present a summary of your research project). Generative AI & MT Statement The written task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Written part to be submitted as a Word file via TurnItIn.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
A maximum extension length of 14 calendar days may be granted on the written submission to support students’ continued progression in their learning and preparation for the subsequent assessment - the follow-up oral discussion in Exam Week 2 .
Students should be aware that any exceptional circumstances extensions exceeding 14 calendar days may lead to delays in scheduling the follow-up interview, which in turn may impact the timely calculation of final results, and therefore, of the final grade in the course not being available by the official grade submission deadline. This is particularly important for students intending to graduate or those who require timely release of results for academic progression or professional purposes.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Part B: Interaction project (Follow-up discussion)
assessment_details.due_date: 15/06/2026 - 19/06/2026 End of Semester EXAM WEEK 2 Students will sign up for the date/time for the follow-up discussion at the end of the teaching period. The student's assigned timeslot will be considered the due date and time for this assessment.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Interaction Project This multi-stage assessment task is designed so that you could apply your knowledge of the pragmatics of social interaction to authentic data – a real-life phone conversation. This task mirrors real-world practices in the fields such as discourse analysis, pragmatics, conversation analysis, interpersonal and intercultural communication where analysing authentic interaction is essential. Interaction Project follow-up oral discussion is a capstone assessment task and pass/fail hurdle requirement for the course . This means that you must complete it in order to pass the course, regardless of your performance in other assessment tasks. Interaction Project follow-up oral discussion (in-person) Deadline : Exam Week 2 Students will need to come to an in-person oral discussion of their submitted written Interaction Project, bring a hard copy of their written submission (with notes, if needed) and, if necessary, original publications used in the submission. No devices will be allowed . The oral examination will take up to 10 minutes, will be audio-recorded and include clarification questions on: (1) Literature review choice and details of the content of readings summarised; (2) Data details; (3) Data analysis claims. IMPORTANT: If you do not come to the project follow-up discussion or fail to demonstrate your knowledge of the written submission and its completion independent of AI tools, the maximum grade that you can receive for the Course will be a 3. Generative AI & MT Statement Part of this assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. The written task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This is an in-person discussion, no submission is necessary.
Students should bring a hard copy of their written submission (with notes, if necessary) and, if necessary, original publications used in the submission. No devices will be allowed.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students should be aware that any extensions to the follow-up discussion may lead to delays in the timely calculation of final results , and therefore, of the final grade in the course not being available by the official grade submission deadline. This is particularly important for students intending to graduate or those who require timely release of results for academic progression or professional purposes.

learning_activities.topic: Tutorials Tutorials provide a space for a detailed exploration of the data relevant to the theoretical concepts explored each week in the readings and lectures. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
